name: The paralytic let down through the roof
notes: A threefold synoptic parallel of the healing of the man sick of the palsy, told by Matthew, Mark, and Luke. The shared core runs the same course in all three: the paralytic brought to Jesus, Jesus seeing their faith and forgiving sins, the scribes and Pharisees reasoning that he blasphemes since God alone forgives, Jesus perceiving their thoughts and posing the question which is easier, the healing wrought that they may know the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, and the crowd's amazement and glory to God. Mark and Luke supply the vivid vantage of the bearers uncovering the roof and letting the man down through the tiling because of the press, a detail Matthew leaves unrecorded as he moves swiftly to the forgiveness and healing. Luke alone notes the Pharisees and doctors of the law gathered from every town and the power of the Lord present to heal, and that the crowd was filled with fear; Mark alone records the man borne of four and the crowd saying they never saw it on this fashion. These complementary vantages enrich the one account.
